title: fragaria palustris
intro: Fragaria palustris, also known as marsh strawberry or bog strawberry, is a species of strawberry native to Northern Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Fragaria palustris include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various ailments such as wounds and infections. The health benefits of consuming Fragaria palustris include reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antihemorrhagic properties. Fragaria palustris can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and its leaves and roots can also be used to make herbal remedies and tinctures.
